actions.title: FinCEN Issues Advisory Urging Financial Institutions to Flag Health Care Fraud Schemes
actions.description: The Treasury Department's Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) issued an advisory urging financial institutions to monitor for fraud targeting Medicare, Medicaid, and other federal and state health care benefit programs. The advisory details how transnational criminal organizations are increasingly exploiting these programs through false claims and money laundering, and provides red flag indicators to help banks identify and report suspicious activity. Financial institutions filed 20% more health care-related suspicious activity reports in 2025 compared to 2024. FinCEN also proposed a rule establishing a whistleblower compensation program for tips related to fraud, money laundering, and sanctions violations.

actions.title: Treasury Secretary Bessent Launches Whistleblower Reward Program for Medicare and Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced a new anti-fraud whistleblower program offering up to 30% of recovered fines to individuals who report Medicare and Medicaid fraud, provided enforcement actions result in penalties exceeding $1 million. The program is administered through FinCEN (fincen.gov/whistleblower) and reward payments come from collected fines, not taxpayer funds. The initiative targets an estimated $70 billion in annual fraud losses and begins enforcement efforts in Minneapolis.

actions.title: Largest-Ever National Health Care Fraud Takedown
actions.description: DOJ charged 324 defendants nationwide for alleged participation in health care fraud schemes targeting Medicare, Medicaid, and other federal programs. Schemes involved more than $14.6 billion in intended losses. CMS reportedly prevented more than $4 billion in fraudulent payments as a result of these efforts.

actions.title: Operation Gold Rush — $10.6 Billion Medicare Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Largest case amount ever charged by DOJ. Members of a transnational criminal organization orchestrated a Medicare fraud scheme totaling more than $10.6 billion. The criminals purchased dozens of medical supply companies in the U.S. and submitted more than $10 billion in fraudulent claims using the stolen identities of more than 1 million Americans.

actions.title: 74 Defendants Charged in Opioid Diversion Schemes
actions.description: As part of the National Health Care Fraud Takedown, DOJ charged 74 defendants across dozens of cases involving the illegal diversion of more than 15 million prescription opioid pills.

actions.title: Arizona: $1.1 Billion Medically Unnecessary Skin Graft Fraud
actions.description: Prosecutors charged 7 defendants, including 5 medical professionals, in connection with approximately $1.1 billion in fraudulent billing involving medically unnecessary skin grafts billed to Medicare and Medicaid.

actions.title: Kentucky Addiction Treatment Center Fraud — $8M+ in False Claims
actions.description: DOJ investigated and prosecuted an addiction treatment fraud scheme at Kentucky Addiction Centers, involving more than $8 million in false claims submitted to Medicare and Kentucky Medicaid. The centers operated clinics in Winchester, Paducah, Paintsville, and London, Kentucky. A federal jury later convicted Dr. José Alzadon, Michael Bregenzer, and Barbie Vanhoose in March 2025 for conspiracy to commit health care fraud, eight counts of health care fraud, and conspiracy to distribute controlled substances. See also the March 20, 2025 conviction entry for full case details.

actions.title: Florida ACA Enrollment Fraud — $233M in Fraudulent Claims
actions.description: DOJ resolved major cases involving fraudulent ACA enrollment schemes in Florida, in which brokers and marketing firms submitted false applications to obtain premium tax credits and subsidies. The cases involved more than $233 million in fraudulent claims, with at least $180 million paid out by the government.

actions.title: Brooklyn Defendants Plead Guilty: $68M Adult Day Care Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: Multiple Brooklyn defendants pleaded guilty in connection with a $68 million adult day care Medicaid fraud scheme. The case involved adult day care centers in Brooklyn billing New York Medicaid for services never provided to beneficiaries. This case was later cited by CMS Administrator Dr. Oz as a prominent example when he launched a 50-question investigation into New York's $124 billion Medicaid program in March 2026, noting patterns of fraud in adult day care programming that significantly exceeded national norms. Prosecuted by the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Eastern District of New York.

actions.title: Florida Nursing Assistant Convicted: $11.4M Medicare Fraud
actions.description: A Florida nursing assistant was convicted for his or her role in an $11.4 million health care fraud scheme targeting Medicare beneficiaries. The conviction, secured by the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of Florida, reflects ongoing DOJ efforts to hold individual health care workers accountable for Medicare fraud, not just corporate entities.

actions.title: New DOJ Division for National Fraud Enforcement Created
actions.description: The administration announced the creation of a new DOJ Division for National Fraud Enforcement, designed to coordinate fraud enforcement across federal programs. The announcement explicitly referenced health care fraud enforcement activity in Minnesota. Health care fraud is expected to be a top priority.

actions.title: Dr. Oz Announces Plans to Audit Minnesota Medicaid; Defer High-Risk Payments
actions.description: CMS Administrator Dr. Oz announced plans to audit Minnesota's Medicaid program and defer payments to high-risk programs, following the massive Minnesota Medicaid fraud scandal. CMS identified approximately 400 Medicaid-billed businesses operating out of a single building that generated approximately $380 million in taxpayer billing.

actions.title: Dr. Oz and Deputy Secretary O'Neill Fact-Finding Mission to Minnesota
actions.description: Dr. Oz and HHS Deputy Secretary Jim O'Neill conducted a fact-finding mission to Minnesota, signaling heightened federal oversight of the state's Medicaid program. O'Neill posted publicly about their findings, emphasizing the administration's focus on uncovering fraud.

actions.title: Dr. Oz Calls on Governor Newsom to Produce Comprehensive CA Anti-Fraud Action Plan
actions.description: Dr. Oz publicly called on California Governor Gavin Newsom to produce a comprehensive action plan to address major fraud in the California health care system.

actions.title: Dr. Oz: Hospice Fraud Spreading Beyond California; CMS Cracking Down
actions.description: CMS Administrator Dr. Mehmet Oz warned that hospice fraud—long concentrated in California—is expanding to other states, and that CMS is intensifying national enforcement. California has been the epicenter of sham hospice fraud, with multiple operators convicted (including the $16M sham hospice ring sentenced in November 2025 and Petros Fichidzhyan sentenced to 12 years). Dr. Oz indicated CMS is using data analytics to identify hospice providers with anomalous billing patterns, expanding scrutiny to Texas, Nevada, and other states where suspicious hospice enrollment has spiked. The CRUSH RFI published later in February 2026 specifically addresses hospice oversight.

actions.title: Trump Plans New Anti-Fraud Task Force for California, Led by JD Vance
actions.description: Reuters reported that President Trump plans to create a new anti-fraud task force focused on California, which will be led by Vice President JD Vance.

actions.title: HHS-OIG Maine Audit: $45.6M Improper Medicaid Payments for Autism Services
actions.description: HHS-OIG reported its Maine audit revealed Maine made at least $45.6 million in improper Medicaid payments for autism services for children. This is part of a series of audits looking into unallowable services and questionable billing patterns in Medicaid ABA services provided to children diagnosed with autism. To date, HHS-OIG has completed three state audits: Indiana, Wisconsin, and Maine.

actions.title: CMS Closes Medicaid Financing Loophole Used by States to Shift Costs to Federal Government
actions.description: CMS announced action to close a Medicaid financing loophole used by states to shift Medicaid costs to the federal government, strengthening program integrity and reducing improper federal spending.

actions.title: CMS Withholds $300M from California Over Unlawful Health Care Spending on Illegal Immigrants
actions.description: Dr. Oz reported CMS will withhold nearly $300 million in Medicaid funding from California because of the scale of its unlawful health care spending on illegal immigrants. CMS found more than $1.8 billion in unlawful health care spending on illegal immigrants across 8 states.
actions.amount: $300M withheld from CA; $1.8B found across 8 states

actions.title: California Must Return $1B+ After CMS Audit on Unlawful Immigrant Health Spending
actions.description: First Assistant U.S. Attorney Bill Essayli reported California must return more than $1 billion to the federal government after a CMS audit uncovered its massive unlawful health care spending on illegal immigrants.

actions.title: HHS Secretary Kennedy: AI Being Used to Detect Medicare Fraud, Potential Billions in Savings
actions.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. claimed the administration is using AI to detect fraud in Medicare, with potential savings of billions of dollars this year.

actions.title: DMERx CEO Gary Cox Convicted of $1B Medicare Fraud Conspiracy
actions.description: A federal jury in the Southern District of Florida convicted Gary Cox, 79, of Maricopa County, Arizona, CEO of Power Mobility Doctor Rx LLC (DMERx), of conspiracy to commit health care fraud and wire fraud, three counts of health care fraud, conspiracy to pay and receive health care kickbacks, and conspiracy to defraud the United States. Cox operated an internet-based platform generating false doctors' orders for orthotic braces and pain creams, targeting hundreds of thousands of Medicare beneficiaries through deceptive mailers, TV ads, and offshore call centers. Medicare paid more than $360 million of the $1 billion+ billed. Cox was sentenced December 19, 2025 to 15 years in prison and ordered to pay $452 million in restitution.

actions.title: Pakistan-Based Operator Indicted in $650M Arizona Medicaid Addiction Treatment Fraud
actions.description: Farrukh Jarar Ali, 41, of Pakistan, was indicted for conspiracy to commit health care fraud in connection with a $650 million scheme involving at least 41 sham substance abuse treatment clinics in Arizona. Ali's Pakistan-based company ProMD Solutions credentialed and enrolled these clinics with Arizona Medicaid (AHCCCS) from April 2021 to July 2023, but the clinics did not provide legitimate care. Patients—many recruited from homeless populations and Native American reservations—were offered kickbacks and free room and board. The clinics billed $650 million and received approximately $564 million in payments.

actions.title: DOJ and HHS Launch Joint False Claims Act Working Group with Six Health Care Enforcement Priorities
actions.description: DOJ and HHS jointly announced the creation of the DOJ-HHS False Claims Act Working Group, co-led by DOJ's Civil Division and the HHS General Counsel, CMS Center for Program Integrity, and HHS-OIG counsel. The group established six priority enforcement areas: Medicare Advantage upcoding, drug/device pricing fraud, patient access barriers, kickback schemes, defective medical devices, and EHR manipulation driving unnecessary utilization. The working group coordinates referrals from HHS to DOJ, cross-agency investigations, and maximizes recoveries.

actions.title: Florida DME Owner Peter Roussonicolos Sentenced to 12 Years for $61.5M Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Peter Roussonicolos, of Port Saint Lucie, Florida, was sentenced to 12 years in prison and 3 years supervised release for conspiring to defraud Medicare through five durable medical equipment supplier companies. Roussonicolos operated the companies as a silent partner while concealing his involvement due to prior felony convictions. The companies submitted approximately $61.5 million in false and fraudulent Medicare claims for medically unnecessary DME and were paid approximately $26.7 million. The scheme involved falsified enrollment forms and kickbacks to patient recruiters.

actions.title: DOJ Launches Health Care Fraud Data Fusion Center Using AI and Cloud Analytics
actions.description: Announced as part of the 2025 National Health Care Fraud Takedown, the DOJ launched the Health Care Fraud Data Fusion Center, bringing together experts from the DOJ Health Care Fraud Unit Data Analytics Team, FBI, HHS-OIG, CMS, DEA, and IRS-CI to detect emerging fraud schemes using cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and advanced analytics. The Center aims to break down inter-agency data silos and increase the efficiency of proactive fraud detection, implementing President Trump's executive order on eliminating federal data silos.

actions.title: CMS Finalizes Marketplace Integrity and Affordability Rule to Combat ACA Enrollment Fraud
actions.description: CMS finalized the Marketplace Integrity and Affordability Final Rule (effective August 25, 2025), targeting the surge of improper ACA exchange enrollments driven by unscrupulous agents and brokers. Key provisions include eliminating the 150% FPL Special Enrollment Period—a major driver of unauthorized enrollments—mandating pre-enrollment verification of SEP eligibility, and adopting a 'preponderance of evidence' standard for terminating agent/broker agreements. CMS estimated the rule will lower individual premiums by approximately 5% on average and save taxpayers up to $12 billion in 2026. CMS suspended 850 agents and brokers in 2024 and estimated approximately 200,000 consumers had plans changed without their consent.

actions.title: CMS and DOGE Find 2.8 Million Americans in Duplicate Medicaid/ACA Enrollments; $14B Annual Savings Identified
actions.description: CMS, in coordination with DOGE, announced the identification of 2.8 million Americans potentially enrolled in duplicate Medicaid/CHIP plans or simultaneously in both Medicaid/CHIP and subsidized ACA Exchange coverage: 1.2 million/month enrolled in Medicaid/CHIP across two or more states and 1.6 million/month enrolled in both Medicaid/CHIP and subsidized Exchange plans. CMS estimated $14 billion in annual taxpayer savings from eliminating duplicate enrollment. CMS directed states to recheck eligibility and provided 30-day notice to affected individuals. HHS Secretary RFK Jr. stated HHS staff 'uncovered millions who were illegally or improperly enrolled.'

actions.title: Troy Health Enters First-Ever Health Care Non-Prosecution Agreement; AI Tool Used to Commit Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Troy Health, Inc. (d/b/a Troy Medicare), a North Carolina-based Medicare Advantage plan, entered into an 18-month Non-Prosecution Agreement (NPA) and agreed to pay $1.43 million—the first NPA under DOJ's updated Corporate Enforcement Policy and the first health care resolution involving misconduct facilitated by artificial intelligence. Troy misused its proprietary AI platform 'Troy.ai' to offer kickbacks to pharmacies for Medicare beneficiary data and to batch-enroll beneficiaries without consent. At the scheme's height, Troy enrolled over 300 beneficiaries in a single day approximately one minute apart.
officials: Matthew R. Galeotti (Acting AAG, Criminal Division)

actions.title: DOJ Expands Health Care Fraud Strike Force to Massachusetts (Boston)
actions.description: The DOJ's Criminal Division announced the expansion of the Health Care Fraud Unit's New England Strike Force into the District of Massachusetts, embedding federal law enforcement—FBI, HHS-OIG, FDA, DEA, HSI, VA-OIG, and IRS-CI—alongside the existing U.S. Attorney's Office Health Care Fraud Unit in Boston. The expansion was driven by Massachusetts and Boston's status as a national hub for health care and life sciences, with particular focus on pharmaceutical/medical device company fraud, provider misconduct, and complex schemes targeting Medicare/Medicaid. In FY2025 alone, Massachusetts recovered over $450 million in coordinated actions.
officials: Matthew R. Galeotti (Acting AAG, Criminal Division)
Leah B. Foley (U.S. Attorney, D. Mass.)

actions.title: True Health Diagnostics CEO, Physicians, and Marketers Pay $6M+ to Settle Lab Kickback Scheme
actions.description: Christopher Grottenthaler, former CEO of True Health Diagnostics, Dr. Hong Davis, Dr. Elizabeth Seymour, and seven marketers agreed to pay a combined $6,068,462 to resolve False Claims Act allegations of a health care kickback scheme. Defendants allegedly offered and paid doctors kickbacks disguised as MSO (Management Service Organization) distributions and consulting fees to induce laboratory testing referrals, submitting false claims to Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE from January 2015 to January 2018. The Eastern District of Texas prosecuted the case.

actions.title: Queens, NY Doctor Alexander Baldonado Sentenced to 7 Years for $24M Medicare Fraud Kickback Scheme
actions.description: Dr. Alexander Baldonado, M.D., 69, of Queens, New York, was sentenced to seven years in prison and ordered to pay $2.21 million in restitution following his conviction at a five-day jury trial in February 2025. Baldonado received illegal cash kickbacks and bribes in exchange for ordering medically unnecessary laboratory tests (including cancer genetic tests) and orthotic braces, submitting over $24 million in fraudulent Medicare claims. Medicare actually paid approximately $2.2 million on these claims.

actions.title: Four California Sham Hospice Operators Sentenced in $16M Medicare Fraud and Money Laundering Scheme
actions.description: Four California defendants were sentenced for their roles in operating sham hospice companies that billed Medicare nearly $16 million for medically unnecessary and never-provided hospice services from July 2019 to January 2023. Sentences: Juan Carlos Esparza (57 months), Karpis Srapyan (57 months), Mihran Panosyan (57 months), and Petros Fichidzhyan (12 years—sentenced separately in April 2025). The scheme used foreign nationals' identities as purported hospice owners and laundered proceeds through shell companies and bank accounts.

actions.title: Done Telehealth CEO and Clinical President Convicted in $100M Adderall Distribution and Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: A federal jury in San Francisco convicted Ruthia He, founder and CEO of Done (California digital health company), and David Brody, its clinical president, for illegally distributing over 40 million Adderall pills online and conspiring to commit health care fraud. The defendants spent $40 million on deceptive social media advertising to attract drug seekers, defrauded insurers of approximately $14 million through false prior authorization requests, and He relocated Done to China to obstruct the DOJ investigation and destroy incriminating documents. He was also convicted of conspiracy to obstruct justice. Sentencing was scheduled for February 25, 2026.

actions.title: Atrium Health and Senior Living (KBWB) and CEO Sentenced for $146M Health Care Fraud and Tax Conspiracy
actions.description: KBWB Operations LLC (d/b/a Atrium Health and Senior Living) and its former CEO/managing member Kevin Breslin, 58, of Hoboken, NJ, were sentenced following December 2024 guilty pleas. Breslin received 90 months in prison; both Breslin and KBWB were ordered to pay $146 million in restitution and $8.4 million in forfeiture. The defendants diverted CMS funds intended for resident care at 23 skilled nursing facilities in New Jersey, Wisconsin, and Michigan—prioritizing owner distributions, diverting employee health insurance premiums and 401(k) contributions, and failing to remit employment taxes to the IRS.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: CMS Put $11.2 Billion at Risk by Not Properly Closing Federal Contracts
actions.description: HHS-OIG Report A-04-24-02048 found that CMS failed to meet administrative closeout requirements for 50 contracts totaling $11.2 billion reviewed for the period October 2018—September 2023. Twelve contracts worth $2.1 billion remained overdue for closeout at audit conclusion, putting those funds at risk of fraud, waste, and abuse. OIG recommended CMS complete administrative closeout for the 12 delinquent contracts and develop clear policies defining documentation requirements and roles for timely closure. CMS concurred with both recommendations.

actions.title: Co-Conspirator Pleads Guilty in $54M California Radiology and Hospice Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Sophia Shaklian, 38, of Los Angeles, pleaded guilty to her role in a $54 million Medicare fraud scheme involving seven health care providers—including radiology imaging centers and a hospice—across Los Angeles County from 2019—2024, billing for services never performed. Shaklian managed and submitted false claims for the providers. Co-conspirator Alex Alexsanian, 48, of Burbank, pleaded guilty to money laundering conspiracy on January 20, 2026, and agreed to forfeit $3 million. The pair pocketed $23 million in total, laundering proceeds through a fake identity and using funds to buy $6 million in gold bars.

actions.title: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute Pays $15M to Settle NIH Research Grant Fraud Allegations
actions.description: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute agreed to pay $15 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that from 2014—2024 it made materially false statements and certifications related to NIH research grants. Specifically, researchers authored papers containing duplicated and manipulated images/data from 14 scientific publications, with six NIH grants funding the fraudulent research and four additional NIH grants obtained through false applications. Welsh microbiologist and whistleblower Sholto David received $2.625 million under qui tam provisions. U.S. Attorney Leah B. Foley noted 'there is no place in scientific research for fraud, waste and abuse.'
officials: Leah B. Foley (U.S. Attorney, D. Mass.)

actions.title: HHS-OIG Medicare Advantage Audit of Humana Louisiana Finds $6.8M in Unsupported Diagnosis Overpayments
actions.description: HHS-OIG published a compliance audit of Humana Health Benefit of Louisiana (Contract H1951) for Medicare Advantage risk adjustment payments for 2017—2018. The audit found that most selected diagnosis codes did not comply with Federal requirements, estimating Humana was overpaid approximately $10 million for the audited contract when extrapolated, with OIG recommending Humana refund $6,777,385 to the Federal Government. Most unsupported diagnoses resulted from past medical conditions improperly reported as current conditions, or from radiology/ECG reports not interpreted and signed by an acceptable provider type.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: Illinois Made $9.5M in Unallowable Medicaid Capitation Payments for Incarcerated Enrollees
actions.description: HHS-OIG Report A-05-24-00019 found Illinois made unallowable capitation payments to managed care organizations for incarcerated Medicaid enrollees who were ineligible for coverage. In a sample of 100 incarcerated enrollees, 48 had unallowable payments totaling $263,186. Extrapolated to the audit period, OIG estimated at least $9.5 million ($8.3 million federal share) in unallowable payments. OIG recommended Illinois refund $8,366,521 in federal funds and expand its automated enrollment termination process to cover inmates outside Illinois DOC facilities. Illinois accepted both recommendations.

actions.title: DMERx CEO Gary Cox Sentenced to 15 Years in Prison; $452M Restitution for $1B Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Gary Cox, 79, of Maricopa County, Arizona, CEO of Power Mobility Doctor Rx LLC (DMERx), was sentenced to 15 years in prison and ordered to pay over $452 million in restitution following his June 2025 conviction for conspiring to defraud Medicare of more than $1 billion. The FBI, HHS-OIG, VA-OIG, and DCIS investigated the case. This represents one of the longest individual sentences for Medicare fraud in recent years.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: Medicaid Agencies Made $207.5M in Unallowable Capitation Payments for Deceased Enrollees
actions.description: HHS-OIG Report A-04-23-09010 estimated that Medicaid agencies made $207.5 million ($138.6 million federal share) in unallowable capitation payments to MCOs for enrollees whose deaths preceded the monthly service periods during FY2022 (July 2021—June 2022). In a sample of 100 payments, 99 were unallowable—49 already not recovered or recovered only after OIG notification. This represents OIG's 18th such audit since 2016, which cumulatively identified approximately $289 million in improper capitation payments for deceased enrollees. OIG recommended CMS provide matched T-MSIS data to states for recovery.

actions.title: Georgia Man Patrick Moore Sentenced to 46 Months for $24M Medicare Genetic Testing Kickback Scheme
actions.description: Patrick C. Moore Jr., of Peachtree City, Georgia, was sentenced to 46 months in prison and ordered to pay $7.2 million in restitution for his role orchestrating a network of recruiters targeting Medicare beneficiaries for medically unnecessary genetic tests. Moore billed approximately $24 million to Medicare and received approximately $4.3 million in illegal kickbacks. He pleaded guilty in May 2025 to one count of conspiracy to defraud the United States and to pay and receive illegal health care kickbacks.

actions.title: Virginia Laboratory NEXT Molecular Analytics Pays $758K+ to Settle Medicare Kickback Allegations
actions.description: NEXT Bio-Research Services LLC (d/b/a NEXT Molecular Analytics), a Chester, Virginia clinical laboratory, agreed to pay at least $758,000 plus contingent amounts to resolve False Claims Act allegations of paying illegal kickbacks to physicians and independent contractor marketers disguised as consulting fees and medical director fees—paid on a per-referral basis—to induce laboratory testing referrals. The kickback scheme involved doctors in Texas and Arkansas.

actions.title: HHS-OIG Spring 2025 Semiannual Report: $16.6B in Health Care Fraud Flagged (Oct 2024—Mar 2025)
actions.description: HHS-OIG's Spring 2025 Semiannual Report to Congress—the first under the current administration—identified more than $16.6 billion in health care fraud, overpayments, and improper payments for the period October 1, 2024 through March 31, 2025. HHS-OIG conducted 946 criminal, civil, and administrative investigations, completed 744 enforcement actions, excluded 1,503 individuals and entities from federal health care programs, made 1,209 criminal referrals, and pursued 395 civil actions. Key focus areas included home health fraud, drug diversion, COVID-19 fraud, and Medicare patient abuse and neglect.

actions.title: HHS-OIG Fall 2025 Semiannual Report: $19.04B in Monetary Impact (Apr—Sep 2025)
actions.description: HHS-OIG's Fall 2025 Semiannual Report to Congress identified $19.04 billion in monetary impact for the period April 1 through September 30, 2025—reflecting a significant increase from the Spring 2025 report's $16.6 billion. The period covered the 2025 National Health Care Fraud Takedown (June 30, 2025) and related enforcement actions. The report covers the peak enforcement period including the $14.6 billion takedown, the $10.6 billion Operation Gold Rush EDNY indictment, and the launch of the Health Care Fraud Data Fusion Center.

actions.title: DOJ Announces Record $6.8B in False Claims Act Recoveries in FY2025; Health Care Accounts for $5.7B
actions.description: DOJ announced that False Claims Act settlements and judgments exceeded $6.8 billion in FY2025 (ending September 30, 2025)—the highest single-year total in FCA history. Over $5.7 billion of that total involved the health care industry, restoring funds to Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE. Whistleblowers filed 1,297 qui tam lawsuits in FY2025 (a new record, surpassing 2024's 980), recovering over $5.3 billion. Since 1986, cumulative FCA recoveries have exceeded $85 billion.

actions.title: CMS FY2025 Improper Payments Fact Sheet: $28.8B Medicare FFS, $23.7B Medicare Advantage
actions.description: CMS published its FY2025 Improper Payments Fact Sheet estimating Medicare Fee-for-Service improper payments at $28.83 billion (6.55% rate), down from $31.70 billion in FY2024. Medicare Advantage (Part C) improper payments rose to $23.67 billion (6.09% rate), up from $19.07 billion, primarily driven by unsupported diagnoses submitted by MA organizations. Medicaid improper payments were $37.39 billion (6.12% rate). CHIP improper payments were $1.37 billion (7.05% rate). CMS noted that most improper payments reflect documentation issues rather than fraud per se.

actions.title: White House Announces New DOJ National Fraud Enforcement Division; Health Care Fraud a Core Focus
actions.description: The Trump White House announced the creation of a new Department of Justice Division for National Fraud Enforcement, to be led by an Assistant Attorney General appointed by the President. The division will enforce federal criminal and civil laws against fraud targeting federal government programs—including Medicare, Medicaid, and other HHS-funded programs—federally funded benefits, businesses, nonprofits, and private citizens. VP J.D. Vance stated the division 'will be run out of the White House.' On January 28, 2026, President Trump announced Colin McDonald as the nominee for AAG of the new division.

actions.title: Kaiser Permanente Affiliates Pay $556M to Resolve Medicare Advantage False Claims Act Allegations
actions.description: Kaiser Foundation Health Plan Inc. and affiliated Kaiser entities in California and Colorado agreed to pay $556 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that Kaiser systematically pressured physicians to alter patient medical records after visits by adding diagnoses not considered or addressed during those visits—mining patients' histories and sending physician 'queries' months or over a year after visits to generate addenda for unsupported diagnoses, and linking physician bonuses to meeting diagnosis goals. This is one of the largest Medicare Advantage risk-adjustment fraud settlements in history.
officials: Scott J. Lampert (HHS-OIG Acting Deputy IG)

actions.title: Five Florida Ophthalmology Practices Pay $6M to Resolve Fraudulent TCD Ultrasound Kickback Claims
actions.description: Five Florida ophthalmology practices—Clay Eye Holdings LLC, Retina Macula Specialist of Miami LLC, Florida Eye Institute P.A., Miami Eye LLC, and Kendall Eye Institute Inc.—agreed to pay nearly $6 million total to resolve False Claims Act allegations of billing Medicare and Medicaid for medically unnecessary trans-cranial Doppler ultrasound (TCD) tests through a kickback arrangement with a third-party testing company. Practices performed TCDs on thousands of patients and pre-labeled them with serious diagnoses (that nearly all patients did not have) before tests were complete. The State of Florida received $333,500 for its Medicaid share; the whistleblower received $1.135 million.
officials: Gregory W. Kehoe (U.S. Attorney, M.D. Fla.)

actions.title: Massachusetts Sleep Doctor Dr. Pankaj Merchia Convicted of Health Care Fraud, Money Laundering, and Tax Evasion
actions.description: A federal jury in Boston convicted Dr. Pankaj Merchia, a Harvard-educated sleep medicine physician, for health care fraud, three counts of money laundering, conspiracy to defraud the IRS, and two counts of tax evasion. Merchia fraudulently billed insurance companies millions of dollars for sleep apnea machines not used by patients for years (2017—2019), billed over $390,000 for fraudulent family member treatment claims, and failed to report over $6.5 million in income (2009—2019) from his medical businesses. He purchased a $2.1 million Massachusetts home with fraud proceeds. Sentencing scheduled for April 28, 2026.

actions.title: CMS Issues Final Rule Closing Medicaid Provider Tax Loophole; $78.2B Decade Savings
actions.description: CMS finalized the 'Preserving Medicaid Funding for Vulnerable Populations—Closing a Health Care-Related Tax Loophole Final Rule,' codifying the Medicaid provider tax provisions adopted by Congress in the Working Families Tax Cuts legislation (Public Law 119-21, signed July 4, 2025). The rule prohibits states from taxing Medicaid business at higher rates than non-Medicaid, blocks indirect tax structures disguising disproportionate Medicaid burdens, and strengthens CMS enforcement authority. CMS estimates the rule saves the federal government $78.2 billion over the next decade, addressing financing gimmicks that drew down over $24 billion annually. Effective April 3, 2026, with phased state compliance deadlines through FY2028.

actions.title: Two Queens, NY Men Charged with $120M Adult Day Care and Pharmacy Medicare/Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: Inwoo Kim (aka 'Tony Kim'), 42, and Daniel Lee (aka 'Daniel Yang'), 56, both of Flushing, Queens, were charged with conspiracy to commit health care fraud. Kim owned a pharmacy and two social adult day care centers—Royal Adult Daycare and Happy Life Inc.—where from 2016—2026 the defendants paid illegal cash bribes and supermarket gift certificates to beneficiaries to fill prescriptions and enroll in day care services, and submitted claims for services never provided. In total, Medicare and Medicaid paid approximately $120 million for medically unnecessary or non-provided prescription drugs and adult day care services.
officials: Scott J. Lampert (HHS-OIG Acting Deputy IG)

actions.title: Two Executives Sentenced to 20 Years Each for $233M ACA Enrollment Fraud Targeting Homeless and Vulnerable
actions.description: Cory Lloyd, 47, of Stuart, Florida, and Steven Strong, 43, of Mansfield, Texas, were each sentenced to 20 years in federal prison and ordered to pay $180.6 million in restitution following their November 2025 jury convictions for a multi-year scheme defrauding the Affordable Care Act subsidy program of approximately $233 million. The defendants used 'street marketers' to fraudulently enroll homeless individuals, people experiencing mental health crises, and substance abuse patients into fully subsidized ACA plans to collect insurance commission payments. A third defendant, Dafud Iza, received 35 months. AG Pam Bondi called the scheme 'evil and unforgivable.'

actions.title: Former NFL Player Keith Gray Convicted in $328M Medicare Genetic Testing Fraud Scheme
actions.description: A federal jury in Dallas convicted Keith J. Gray, 39, of McKinney, Texas—a former NFL/Carolina Panthers player—and owner of Axis Professional Labs LLC and Kingdom Health Laboratory LLC, for conspiracy to defraud the United States, conspiracy to pay/receive health care kickbacks (five AKS counts), and three counts of money laundering. Gray billed Medicare approximately $328 million (Medicare paid ~$54 million) for medically unnecessary cardiovascular genetic tests by paying kickbacks to marketers for patient DNA samples, Medicare numbers, and signed physician orders. Gray laundered proceeds through luxury vehicle purchases.

actions.title: Texas Orthopedic Surgeon Dr. Michael Taba Sentenced to 8.5 Years for $145M DOL Workers' Comp Prescription Fraud
actions.description: Dr. Michael Taba, of McKinney, Texas, was sentenced to 102 months (8.5 years) in prison and ordered to pay over $13 million in restitution following his November 2023 conviction for conspiracy to commit health care fraud and three counts of health care fraud. Taba accepted bribes from pharmacy owners to prescribe medically unnecessary compound creams to injured federal workers. From May 2014 to March 2017, pharmacies billed the Department of Labor's Office of Workers' Compensation Programs and Blue Cross Blue Shield more than $145 million and received more than $90 million for Taba-referred prescriptions.

actions.title: Arizona OASIS Hospital/USPI Pay $5.6M to Resolve Improper Financial Arrangement with Physicians
actions.description: Southwest Orthopedic and Spine Hospital LLC (d/b/a OASIS Hospital), United Surgical Partners International Inc. (USPI), and an affiliated entity agreed to pay $5.6 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that OASIS made improper interest payments on convertible bonds to a physician group that referred patients to OASIS, violating both the Anti-Kickback Statute and the Stark Law (Physician Self-Referral Law) from 2011—2018.
officials: Scott J. Lampert (HHS-OIG Acting Deputy IG)

actions.title: CMS/HHS Announce Major Anti-Fraud Package: $259.5M MN Deferral, DMEPOS Moratorium, CRUSH Initiative
actions.description: Vice President J.D. Vance, HHS Secretary RFK Jr., and CMS Administrator Dr. Oz jointly announced a sweeping anti-fraud package at the White House. Actions included: (1) $259.5 million deferral of quarterly federal Medicaid matching funds to Minnesota ($243.8M for fraudulent claims; $15.4M for unauthorized immigrant claims); (2) a nationwide six-month moratorium on new Medicare DMEPOS supplier enrollment, building on $1.5 billion in prevented DMEPOS billing in 2025; (3) the CRUSH (Comprehensive Regulations to Uncover Suspicious Health Care) initiative RFI published in the Federal Register; (4) CMS-State Tax Fraud Partnership with 28 states. CMS metrics for 2025: $5.7 billion suspended, 122,658 claims denied, 5,586 providers revoked, 372 fraud referrals totaling $3.7 billion.

actions.title: Atlanta Gastroenterology Associates Pays $4.75M to Settle Kickbacks and Unnecessary Testing Allegations
actions.description: Atlanta Gastroenterology Associates (Georgia) and Advanced Pathology Solutions (Arkansas) agreed to pay $4.75 million combined to resolve False Claims Act allegations that Atlanta Gastroenterology received kickbacks in exchange for patient referrals to Advanced Pathology Solutions, and that APS performed medically unnecessary gastrointestinal pathology testing on those patients through automatic 'reflex ordering' without medical justification.
officials: Jonathan D. Ross (U.S. Attorney, E.D. Ark.)

actions.title: CMS Publishes CRUSH RFI in Federal Register; Seeks Public Input on Sweeping Anti-Fraud Regulation
actions.description: CMS published a Request for Information (RFI) in the Federal Register as part of its Comprehensive Regulations to Uncover Suspicious Health Care (CRUSH) initiative, signaling a forthcoming major anti-fraud rulemaking. The RFI covers over a dozen subject areas including enhanced provider enrollment screening, identity proofing, preclusion list reforms, laboratory test fraud, DMEPOS supplier oversight, claim filing deadlines, AI-assisted coding oversight, beneficiary solicitation protections, surety bonds, Medicaid and CHIP fraud, and Marketplace program integrity. Public comments due March 30, 2026.

actions.title: Dr. Oz Launches Medicaid Fraud Investigation of New York; Sends 50-Question Letter to Gov. Hochul
actions.description: CMS Administrator Dr. Mehmet Oz sent a letter to New York Gov. Kathy Hochul posing 50 detailed questions about fraud, waste, and abuse in New York's $124 billion Medicaid program—the largest state Medicaid program in the country. Oz cited serious concerns about New York's oversight of personal care, home health, adult day care programming, non-emergency medical transportation, and behavioral health services, noting per-capita spending and workforce utilization patterns significantly exceeding national norms. Hochul was given 30 days to respond with a corrective action plan or face potential deferral of federal Medicaid payments. CMS cited the $68 million Brooklyn adult day care fraud as an example.

actions.title: Independent Health Agrees to Pay Up to $98M to Settle Medicare Advantage Upcoding Allegations
actions.description: Independent Health Association and Independent Health Corporation (Buffalo, NY) agreed to pay up to $98 million—$34.5 million guaranteed plus up to $63.5 million contingent—to resolve False Claims Act allegations that they submitted or caused the submission of invalid diagnosis codes to Medicare for Medicare Advantage enrollees to increase risk-adjusted payments. Co-defendant DxID LLC (a risk adjustment vendor) and its CEO Betsy Gaffney separately agreed to pay $2 million. Independent Health entered a five-year corporate integrity agreement with HHS-OIG requiring independent review of MA patients' medical records annually.

actions.title: C.R. Bard & Affiliates Pay $17M for Catheter Kickback Scheme
actions.description: C.R. Bard, Inc. and affiliates Liberator Medical Supply, Liberator Holdings, and Rochester Medical Corporation agreed to pay $17 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that they provided free samples and discounts to urology practice groups as illegal kickbacks in exchange for using Bard's proprietary 'Link' prescription form for intermittent catheters. The scheme ran from 2016 through February 2020. A former Bard employee filed the qui tam whistleblower lawsuit.

actions.title: Pfizer (Biohaven) Pays $59.7M for Physician Kickbacks via Speaker Programs on Nurtec ODT
actions.description: Pfizer Inc., on behalf of subsidiary Biohaven Pharmaceutical, agreed to pay $59,746,277 to resolve False Claims Act and Anti-Kickback Statute allegations. From March 2020 through September 2022, Biohaven paid physician speaker honoraria and provided lavish meals at high-end restaurants to induce prescriptions of the migraine drug Nurtec ODT. Programs included attendees with no legitimate educational need such as family members, and some prescribers attended duplicate sessions with no educational value.

actions.title: Jeffrey King (Apex Medical) Pleads Guilty to $1.2B Wound Graft Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Jeffrey King, 46, of Phoenix, Arizona, co-owner of Apex Medical LLC and Viking Medical Consultants LLC, pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit health care fraud for his role in a $1.2 billion scheme involving medically unnecessary amniotic wound grafts applied to elderly and hospice patients. King and co-conspirators instructed untrained sales reps to order grafts in maximum sizes regardless of wound size to maximize reimbursement. They submitted $1,212,005,778 in false claims — over $960 million targeting Medicare, TRICARE, and CHAMPVA.

actions.title: California Man Pleads Guilty to $17M Sham Hospice and Home Health Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Petros Fichidzhyan, 43, of Granada Hills, California, pleaded guilty to health care fraud, aggravated identity theft, and money laundering for orchestrating a years-long scheme to defraud Medicare of more than $17 million through sham hospice companies and a home health care agency. Fichidzhyan and co-schemers used stolen identities of foreign nationals as purported owners of the hospices and submitted false claims for services not provided. He also wrongfully obtained over $1 million for a home health agency by fraudulently using a doctor's identity.

actions.title: Medisca Inc. Pays $21.75M for Inflated AWPs on Compounded Prescription Ingredients
actions.description: Compound ingredient supplier Medisca Inc. agreed to pay $21.75 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that it knowingly inflated Average Wholesale Prices (AWPs) for two compound prescription ingredients — resveratrol and mometasone furoate — to increase reimbursements its pharmacy customers received from federal health care programs including TRICARE and DOL Workers' Compensation. Medisca bought resveratrol for ~$0.37/gram and reported an AWP of $777/gram. A compounding pharmacist whistleblower will receive $3.4 million.

actions.title: Saint Vincent's Catholic Medical Centers (NY) Pays $29M for Retaining Military Health Care Overpayments
actions.description: SVCMC Inc. (formerly Saint Vincents Catholic Medical Centers of New York) agreed to pay $29 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that it knowingly retained erroneously inflated payments from the Department of Defense for health care services provided to retired military members and their families. Saint Vincent's allegedly learned of calculation errors resulting in overpayments in 2012 but failed to notify the government or repay the funds, continuing to submit invoices at the inflated rates.

actions.title: Health Net Federal Services & Centene Pay $11.25M for Cybersecurity False Claims (TRICARE)
actions.description: Health Net Federal Services LLC and its parent Centene Corporation agreed to pay $11,253,400 to resolve False Claims Act allegations stemming from cybersecurity failures. From 2015 through 2018, HNFS allegedly failed to meet cybersecurity controls and falsely certified compliance in annual reports to the Defense Health Agency under its TRICARE contract, including failing to timely scan for known vulnerabilities and remediate security flaws on its networks.

actions.title: DMERx VP Gregory Schreck Pleads Guilty to $1B Medicare Fraud Conspiracy
actions.description: Gregory Schreck, 50, Vice President of Power Mobility Doctor Rx LLC (DMERx), pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit health care fraud. Schreck admitted he and co-conspirators used the DMERx internet platform to generate false doctors' orders, connecting DME suppliers and pharmacies with telemedicine companies that accepted illegal kickbacks in exchange for signed orders. The DME suppliers and pharmacies paid kickbacks and billed Medicare and other insurers more than $1 billion, of which Medicare paid more than $360 million.

actions.title: Three Convicted for $8M Medicare/Medicaid Fraud Through Kentucky Opioid Addiction Clinics
actions.description: A federal jury convicted Dr. José Alzadon, Michael Bregenzer, and Barbie Vanhoose for their roles in a scheme to fraudulently bill Medicare and Kentucky Medicaid for over $8 million through Kentucky Addiction Centers, operating clinics in Winchester, Paducah, Paintsville, and London. Each defendant was convicted of conspiracy to commit health care fraud, eight counts of health care fraud, and conspiracy to distribute controlled substances.

actions.title: Seoul Medical Group Pays $62.85M for Medicare Advantage Diagnosis Upcoding
actions.description: Seoul Medical Group, its subsidiary Advanced Medical Management, the former president, and an affiliated radiology group collectively agreed to pay over $62.85 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that they fraudulently increased Medicare Advantage reimbursements by submitting unsupported diagnosis codes for severe spinal conditions for patients who did not have those conditions.

actions.title: Diopsys Inc. Pays Up to $14.25M for Off-Label Vision Device Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Diopsys Inc. agreed to pay up to $14.25 million to resolve False Claims Act and state law allegations that the company caused health care providers to submit false claims to Medicare and Medicaid for its NOVA device used for electroretinography testing — an indication for which the device lacked FDA clearance — between January 2015 and December 2021. The case arose from a qui tam suit by a California ophthalmologist.

actions.title: DOJ Files FCA Complaint Against Vohra Wound Physicians for Medicare Overbilling in Nursing Homes
actions.description: The Department of Justice filed a False Claims Act complaint against Vohra Wound Physicians Management LLC, its founder Dr. Ameet Vohra, and VHS Holdings PA. The complaint alleges Vohra engaged in a nationwide scheme to bill Medicare for medically unnecessary and upcoded wound care services in skilled nursing facilities, using proprietary software that automatically billed higher-reimbursed surgical debridements and corporate revenue quotas that pressured physicians to perform unnecessary procedures.

actions.title: Walgreens Agrees to Pay Up to $350M for Illegally Filling Opioid Prescriptions and False Medicare Claims
actions.description: The Department of Justice, DEA, and HHS-OIG announced that Walgreens Boots Alliance agreed to pay up to $350 million to resolve allegations that Walgreens knowingly filled millions of unlawful controlled substance prescriptions from August 2012 through March 2023 and submitted false claims to federal health care programs. The government alleged Walgreens pressured pharmacists to fill prescriptions quickly without proper verification. The settlement includes a seven-year DEA compliance agreement and a five-year HHS-OIG Corporate Integrity Agreement.

actions.title: Gilead Sciences Pays $202M for Illegal Speaker Program Kickbacks on HIV Drugs
actions.description: The U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York announced a $202 million settlement with Gilead Sciences to resolve False Claims Act and Anti-Kickback Statute allegations. Between 2011 and 2017, Gilead paid 548 health care providers more than $23.7 million in honoraria, meals, and travel through sham speaker programs to induce prescriptions of HIV medications. The settlement provides $49 million for Medicaid programs nationwide, with the remainder going to Medicare, TRICARE, and the AIDS Drug Assistance Program.

actions.title: DOJ Files FCA Complaint Against Aetna, Humana, Elevance & Brokers for $100Ms in Medicare Advantage Kickbacks
actions.description: The Department of Justice filed a False Claims Act complaint against three of the nation's largest Medicare Advantage insurers — Aetna, Elevance Health (formerly Anthem), and Humana — and three major brokers — eHealth, GoHealth, and SelectQuote — alleging they paid and received hundreds of millions of dollars in illegal kickbacks for Medicare Advantage enrollment steering from 2016 through at least 2021. Aetna and Humana also allegedly conspired to discriminate against Medicare beneficiaries with disabilities, pressuring brokers to enroll fewer disabled beneficiaries perceived as less profitable.

actions.title: California Hospice Fraudster Petros Fichidzhyan Sentenced to 12 Years for $17M Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Petros Fichidzhyan, 44, of Granada Hills, California was sentenced to 12 years in prison and ordered to pay $17,129,060 in restitution for operating sham hospice companies and a fraudulent home health care agency that defrauded Medicare of more than $17 million. Fichidzhyan and co-conspirators used stolen identities of foreign nationals as purported hospice owners and billed for services not provided. Two homes purchased with fraud proceeds were forfeited.

actions.title: DOJ Criminal Division Names Health Care Fraud Top Enforcement Priority in New White-Collar Plan
actions.description: DOJ's Criminal Division head Matthew Galeotti announced the department's first-ever White-Collar Enforcement Plan, explicitly naming 'waste, fraud, and abuse, including health care fraud and federal program fraud' as the number one enforcement priority. The plan also updated the Corporate Enforcement Policy, revised monitor selection guidance, and expanded the DOJ Whistleblower Pilot Program to cover tips about health care fraud involving public health care programs and fraud against patients and private insurers.

actions.title: Fresno Community Hospital Pays $31.5M for Anti-Kickback and Stark Law Violations
actions.description: Fresno Community Hospital and Medical Center and its technology affiliate Physicians Network Advantage Inc. agreed to pay $31.5 million and enter a five-year Corporate Integrity Agreement to resolve False Claims Act, Anti-Kickback Statute, and Stark Law allegations. The hospital allegedly provided improper inducements to referring physicians including meals, alcohol, cigars, and substantial EHR software subsidies beginning in 2013. A whistleblower will receive approximately $5 million.

actions.title: Cuban National Julian Lopez Sentenced for $3.2M Miami Medicare DME Fraud
actions.description: Julian Lopez, 55, a Cuban citizen residing in Miami-Dade County, Florida, was sentenced to 30 months in prison and ordered to pay $1.5 million in restitution for his role in a $3.2 million Medicare fraud scheme. Lopez obtained Medicare beneficiary identification cards and sold the personal information to a durable medical equipment company, which billed Medicare for orthotic braces never provided to beneficiaries.

actions.title: Massachusetts DME Owner Raju Sharma Pleads Guilty to $30M Medicare Telemedicine Fraud
actions.description: Raju Sharma, 61, of Sharon, Massachusetts, owner of Pharmagears LLC and RR Medco LLC, agreed to plead guilty to conspiracy to commit health care fraud for his role in a nearly $30 million fraud scheme. Between February 2021 and February 2025, Sharma contracted with telemarketing companies to contact Medicare beneficiaries and generate orders for medically unnecessary orthotics via brief telemedicine encounters. His companies billed Medicare approximately $29.6 million and were paid approximately $15.8 million. Sharma used proceeds to purchase three Rolex watches, two Ferraris, and a Mercedes-Benz.

actions.title: CMS Expands Medicare Advantage RADV Audits to All ~550 Plans; Grows Auditor Workforce 50x
actions.description: CMS announced a major expansion of Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) audits targeting Medicare Advantage plans, expanding from approximately 60 plans to all eligible MA contracts (approximately 550 plans). Records audited per plan will increase from 35 to between 35 and 200. CMS will grow its medical coding workforce from 40 to approximately 2,000 by September 1, 2025, deploy advanced record-review technology, and complete all outstanding audits for payment years 2018—2024 by early 2026. Federal estimates suggest MA plans may overbill by $17—43 billion annually.

actions.title: Chesapeake Regional Medical Center Indicted for Health Care Fraud — Rare Hospital Indictment
actions.description: A federal grand jury in the Eastern District of Virginia indicted Chesapeake Regional Medical Center for conspiracy to commit health care fraud and obstruction in connection with enabling Dr. Javaid Perwaiz to perform thousands of medically unnecessary surgeries (hysterectomies, elective inductions) from 2010—2019. CRMC allegedly received approximately $18.5 million in reimbursements from Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE for these procedures. This is among the rarest instances of a hospital entity — rather than individuals — being criminally charged for health care fraud.

actions.title: Pennsylvania Brothers Convicted of Decades Long Racketeering Conspiracy
actions.description: Three individuals were convicted today at trial in connection with a racketeering conspiracy encompassing multiple fraud schemes, including visa fraud to employ foreign workers unlawfully, soliciting salary kickbacks from the employed workers, and health care fraud billing schemes resulting in over $32 million in losses to Pennsylvania Medicaid.

actions.title: Senate Report: UnitedHealth 'Aggressively' Gaming Medicare Advantage, Turning Risk Adjustment Into Profit Engine
actions.description: Sen. Grassley's 105-page Senate Judiciary Committee report, based on 50,000+ pages of internal UnitedHealth documents, finds the nation's largest MA insurer (10M members) systematically transformed diagnosis coding into a 'major profit-centered strategy.' UnitedHealth guides providers to apply lucrative diagnosis codes, including diagnosing patients taking prescribed opioids as physically dependent. Industry-wide MA upcoding is estimated to cost taxpayers $84B/year. DOJ has opened both criminal and civil fraud investigations into UnitedHealth's billing practices.

actions.title: Aetna Agrees to Pay $117.7 Million to Resolve False Claims Act Allegations
actions.description: Aetna Inc. agreed to pay $117.7 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that it submitted inaccurate diagnosis codes to Medicare to inflate risk adjustment payments. Aetna operated a chart review program that identified unsupported diagnoses but failed to withdraw them, and submitted false morbid obesity diagnoses (2018-2023) for beneficiaries whose BMI readings did not support such coding. The settlement resolves allegations spanning payment years 2015 and 2018-2023. The case was brought by whistleblower Mary Melette Thomas, a former Aetna risk-adjustment coding auditor.

actions.title: Denver Post: Zynex Executives Indicted in $873 Million Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Federal grand jury indicted Thomas Sandgaard (CEO) and Anna Lucsok (COO) of Zynex Inc. for a 2017-2025 scheme to ship excessive and unnecessary medical supplies to patients and fraudulently bill insurers. The scheme generated $873 million+ in revenue ($600M+ for supplies alone). Assets including luxury cars, a private jet, and properties were seized.

actions.title: House Energy & Commerce Hearing: Common Schemes, Real Harm — Examining Medicare/Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: The Oversight and Investigations Subcommittee held its first major hearing on Medicare/Medicaid fraud trends, examining how transnational crime organizations are increasingly involved. Expert witnesses testified on fraud schemes nationwide. The committee subsequently expanded its investigation to 10 additional states (CA, CO, MA, ME, NE, NY, OR, PA, VT, WA). Estimated annual Medicare/Medicaid fraud losses cited at $100 billion+.

actions.title: GAO Report: 96% of Fictitious ACA Marketplace Applications Approved; $94M+ in Subsidies Tied to Deceased
actions.description: GAO created 20 fictitious identities and submitted 24 applications; 23 of 24 were approved. 18 of 20 fictitious enrollees remained actively covered. Found 68,000 SSNs in 2024 used to receive more than one year of coverage. Over 58,000 SSNs receiving subsidies matched death records ($94M+ in APTC). Total APTC paid was nearly $124 billion for 19.5 million enrollees in plan year 2024.
actions.amount: $124 billion total APTC; $94 million+ tied to deceased

actions.title: Denver Post: Zynex Admits to Fraud, Agrees to Forfeit $98 Million in DOJ Settlement
actions.description: Zynex Inc. entered a non-prosecution agreement with DOJ, admitting to conspiracy to commit health care fraud, securities fraud, and mail fraud. Zynex agreed to forfeit $98 million in billings ($85M from TRICARE, $13M from other payors) and pay $5M-$12.5M in penalties depending on future earnings.

actions.title: House Oversight Hearing: Oversight of Fraud and Misuse of Federal Funds in Minnesota — Part I
actions.description: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform held first hearing on Minnesota Medicaid fraud. Federal prosecutors estimate up to $9 billion stolen from 14 Minnesota Medicaid programs. Witnesses included Minnesota state legislators from the Fraud Prevention Committee. Feeding Our Future scandal alone involved ~$300 million stolen. Fraud identified in autism centers, sober homes, transportation, adult day services, and assisted living.

actions.title: House Oversight Hearing: Curbing Federal Fraud — Examining Innovative Detection Tools
actions.description: House Oversight Subcommittee on Government Operations examined innovative tools to detect and prevent fraud in federal programs. GAO estimates the federal government loses $231-521 billion annually to fraud. Witnesses included PRAC Executive Director and GAO Chief Scientist. Discussion of transitioning pandemic fraud detection infrastructure to government-wide use.

actions.title: House E&C and Ways & Means: Health Insurance CEO Hearings on Costs, MA Upcoding, and Vertical Integration
actions.description: Back-to-back hearings before House Energy & Commerce and Ways & Means committees with CEOs of UnitedHealth Group, CVS Health, Elevance Health, Cigna Group, and Blue Shield. Bipartisan questioning on rising premiums, prior authorization denials, vertical integration, and Medicare Advantage upcoding. Chair Jason Smith questioned extent of marketplace consolidation.

actions.title: Senate HELP Committee Launches Task Force to Eliminate Fraud in Federal Spending
actions.description: Senate HELP Committee Chair Bill Cassidy launched bipartisan task force to eliminate fraud in federal spending, including health care. Health prong led by Sens. Ashley Moody (FL) and Roger Marshall (KS). Task force formed in response to Minnesota fraud scandal; estimates up to 10% of the U.S. budget is stolen annually by fraudsters.

actions.title: HSGAC Subcommittee Hearing: Examining Fraud and Foreign Influence in State and Federal Programs
actions.description: Senate Homeland Security subcommittee examined foreign actors exploiting state and federal programs including Medicaid. Minnesota fraud used as case study. Witnesses included Minnesota State Sen. Mark Koran, LexisNexis Risk Solutions CEO, and government accountability experts. Republicans outlined fraud schemes in Minnesota, Arizona, and California.

actions.title: House Oversight Hearing: Minnesota Fraud Part II — Governor Walz and AG Ellison Testify
actions.description: House Oversight Committee held second hearing on Minnesota Medicaid fraud with testimony from Gov. Tim Walz and AG Keith Ellison. Committee interim staff report alleged Walz and Ellison were aware of widespread fraud since 2019, misled the public, and retaliated against whistleblowers. VP Vance announced temporary pause on $250+ million in Medicaid funding to Minnesota.

actions.title: House E&C Expands Medicaid Fraud Investigation to 10 Additional States
actions.description: House Energy & Commerce Committee leaders sent letters to 10 additional states (CA, CO, MA, ME, NE, NY, OR, PA, VT, WA) requesting information on Medicaid fraud and program integrity measures. Expansion follows the Feb 3 hearing on Medicare and Medicaid fraud schemes and ongoing Minnesota investigation.

actions.title: Presidential Memorandum: Eliminating Waste, Fraud, and Abuse in Medicaid
actions.description: President Trump issued a Presidential Memorandum directing HHS and CMS to eliminate waste, fraud, and abuse in Medicaid. Orders alignment of Medicaid payment rates with Medicare rates where legally possible. Targets state-directed payment programs viewed as inflating reimbursement beyond Medicare levels.

actions.title: One Big Beautiful Bill Act Signed Into Law: $185B in Health Care Fraud Savings, Medicaid/ACA Overhaul
actions.description: H.R. 1 signed into law with sweeping health care fraud and program integrity provisions. Requires full ACA eligibility verification before premium tax credits; eliminates exploited special enrollment periods (CBO: 2.3M improper enrollees). Medicaid work requirements (80 hrs/month), six-month redeterminations, quarterly SSA Death Master File checks, cross-state duplicate enrollment prevention, and 3% error rate penalty for states. Invests $25M in AI fraud detection for Medicare. CBO estimates $185B in savings from fraud reforms; $900B+ in Medicaid changes over 10 years.

actions.title: New Jersey Expands Patient Brokering and Anti-Kickback Statute to Labs and Recovery Homes
actions.description: Acting Governor Way signed legislation expanding New Jersey's patient brokering statute to cover clinical laboratories and recovery residences. Prohibits kickbacks, bribes, and in-kind gifts in connection with patient referrals. Elevates kickback offense to third-degree crime: 3-5 years prison, $50,000 fine, plus restitution. DOH and DCA can suspend/revoke licenses and impose civil fines up to $20,000 per violation.

actions.title: NDAA FY2025 Creates Administrative False Claims Act: Raises Agency Fraud Threshold to $1M
actions.description: Section 5203 of the National Defense Authorization Act (P.L. 118-159, signed Dec 23, 2024) renames and revitalizes the Program Fraud Civil Remedies Act as the Administrative False Claims Act. Raises the ceiling for claims agencies can handle administratively from $150,000 to $1,000,000. Expands to include reverse false claims. Extends statute of limitations to 6 years after violation or 3 years after discovery (10-year cap). Gives HHS, CMS, and other agencies significantly more power to pursue health care fraud cases without DOJ litigation.

actions.title: Consolidated Appropriations Act 2026 Signed: PBM Transparency Reform and Anti-Fraud Provisions
actions.description: H.R. 7148 signed into law with major pharmacy benefit manager (PBM) reform. Beginning 2028, PBMs prohibited from deriving remuneration from rebates, spread pricing, or volume-based arrangements; only flat-fee bona fide service fees permitted. Mandates semiannual PBM reporting on drug spending and rebates. Makes it unlawful to submit false or misleading pharmacy benefit information to federal agencies. PBMs liable for civil monetary penalties. Prohibits reimbursement clawbacks unless original claim was fraudulent. $321M+ earmarked for drug cost transparency.

actions.title: Oregon Passes SB 1575: Hospice Fraud Prevention with License Moratorium and Background Checks
actions.description: Oregon SB 1575 passed both chambers (Senate 19-8, House 52-1) in direct response to the California hospice fraud crisis. Imposes moratorium on new hospice licenses until Oregon Health Authority updates rules. Bars hospice operators with history of fraud or substandard care in other states from opening in Oregon. Requires background checks for hospice owners. Awaiting Governor Kotek's signature.

actions.title: Executive Order: Combating Cybercrime, Fraud, and Predatory Schemes Against American Citizens
actions.description: President Trump signed executive order creating a coordination cell within the National Coordination Center for cybercrime detection and disruption. Orders 60-day interagency review of frameworks for combating transnational cybercrime and 120-day action plan to identify and dismantle criminal networks. Establishes Victim Restoration Program for restitution from seized cybercriminal funds. Addresses cyber-enabled health care billing fraud conducted by transnational criminal organizations.

actions.title: Senate Finance Chair Crapo: Billions Wasted Due to Health Care Fraud in Obamacare Plans
actions.description: Senate Finance Committee Chairman Mike Crapo responded to the GAO report on ACA marketplace fraud, highlighting failures allowing applicants using fake identities, stolen SSNs, and incorrect income estimates to receive subsidies at taxpayers' expense. Called the enhanced subsidies a driver of fraud vulnerability and signaled intent for legislative action.

actions.title: House Judiciary Joint Hearing: Fighting Obamacare Subsidy Fraud — Is the APA Working as Intended?
actions.description: Joint hearing of House Judiciary Subcommittees on Oversight and Administrative State examined ACA subsidy fraud following the GAO report. Expert witnesses from Johns Hopkins, GAO, Paragon Institute, and Yale testified. Examined how enhanced COVID-era subsidies created environment for waste, fraud, and abuse. Discussed whether the Administrative Procedure Act functioned as intended.

actions.title: House Judiciary Launches Probe of 8 Major Insurers Over ACA Enrollment Fraud
actions.description: House Judiciary Committee sent demand letters to Blue Shield of California, Centene, CVS Health/Aetna, Elevance Health, GuideWell, Health Care Service Corporation, Kaiser Permanente, and Oscar Health demanding documents about ACA subsidy fraud and unauthorized broker enrollment practices.

actions.title: KFF Health News: Exactech Pays $8M to Settle FCA Allegations Over Defective Knee Implants Sold to Medicare/VA
actions.description: Gainesville, FL-based Exactech agreed to pay $8 million to settle False Claims Act allegations that it knowingly sold defective knee replacement components to Medicare, Medicaid, and VA patients. The company knew by 2008 that its Optetrak knee system failed prematurely at unacceptable rates but continued selling through 2018. Over 2,500 patients alleging injuries filed claims; many needed revision surgeries within three years. Case brought by whistleblower former sales agents and a surgeon.

actions.title: Federal Jury Convicts Three Women for Conspiracy to Commit Wire Fraud Related to Rocky Hill Pharmacy
actions.description: Three women were convicted by a federal jury in connection with a pharmacy-related wire fraud conspiracy involving health care billing.

actions.title: Arizona Cardiology Group to Pay $4.75M to Resolve Allegations of Unnecessary Vein Ablations
actions.description: An Arizona cardiology group agreed to pay $4.75 million to settle allegations that it performed medically unnecessary vascular vein ablation procedures and billed federal health care programs.

actions.title: Former Greenville CEO and Employees Indicted in Multi-Million Dollar Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: A former CEO and employees of a Greenville health care facility were indicted for their roles in a multi-million dollar health care fraud scheme involving COVID-19 pandemic benefits.

actions.title: Chiropractor Sentenced to 43 Months in Prison for $14.9 Million Health Care Fraud and Kickback Scheme
actions.description: A chiropractor was sentenced to 43 months in federal prison for orchestrating a $14.9 million health care fraud and illegal kickback scheme involving durable medical equipment and genetic testing.

actions.title: Psychiatrist Reaches $360,000 Civil Settlement to Resolve Allegations of False Claims
actions.description: A psychiatrist agreed to pay $360,000 to settle civil allegations of submitting false billing claims to federal health care programs.

actions.title: Owner of Durable Medical Equipment Company Sentenced for $59 Million Medicare Fraud
actions.description: The owner of a durable medical equipment company was sentenced to federal prison for perpetrating a $59 million Medicare fraud scheme involving fraudulent DME billing.

actions.title: AmerisourceBergen Subsidiary Agrees to Pay $1 Million for Allegedly Paying Kickbacks
actions.description: A subsidiary of AmerisourceBergen agreed to pay $1 million to resolve allegations of paying unlawful inducements (kickbacks) to health care providers and practice executives.

actions.title: Illinois Man Sentenced to Two Years in Prison for Durable Medical Equipment Fraud Scheme
actions.description: An Illinois man was sentenced to two years in federal prison for operating a fraudulent durable medical equipment billing scheme targeting Medicare.

actions.title: Kansas Doctor Sentenced to 3 Years in Prison for $8 Million Medicare Fraud
actions.description: A Kansas physician was sentenced to three years in federal prison for perpetrating an $8 million Medicare fraud scheme.

actions.title: Man Sentenced to 14 Years in Federal Prison for Leading a $6.85 Million Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: A man was sentenced to 14 years in federal prison for leading a $6.85 million health care fraud operation targeting federal health programs.

actions.title: DOJ: Oklahoma Medical Supply Company Owner Indicted for $30M Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Mark Loftis, 38, of Cushing, OK, owner of Back Pain Home Supplies LLC (EZ Medical Supply), indicted for paying illegal kickbacks to marketers and telemedicine companies for fraudulent DME orders. Submitted approximately $30 million in false claims; federal programs paid approximately $17 million. Also misused $300K+ in COVID-19 Provider Relief Funds for personal expenses.

actions.title: DOJ: 'Fraud Tourists' Plead Guilty to $4.5M Minneapolis Medicaid Fraud Using AI-Fabricated Records
actions.description: Anthony Waddell Jefferson, 37, and Lester Brown, 53, of Philadelphia, pleaded guilty to repeatedly traveling to Minneapolis to defraud Minnesota's Housing Stabilization Services program of $3.5M. They recruited homeless individuals at shelters, billed for services never provided, and used ChatGPT to fabricate client records when questioned. First charges involving AI to further health care fraud in Minnesota.

actions.title: DOJ: Two Foreign Nationals Indicted in Chicago as Part of $10M Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Burhan Mirza, 31, and Kashif Iqbal, 48, both Pakistani nationals, indicted for using fake medical businesses to submit $10 million in fraudulent billing to Medicare and private insurers. Mirza obtained patient identifying information without their knowledge; Iqbal managed DME providers submitting false claims and laundered fraud proceeds.

actions.title: DOJ: Russian Citizen Charged with Laundering $12.2M Connected to $400M in Fraudulent Medicare Claims
actions.description: Nikolai Buzolin, 38, of Russia, charged with conspiracy to commit money laundering in connection with Verisola Inc., which submitted $400M+ in false Medicare Advantage claims for DME (orthotic braces, glucose monitors) never provided to patients. MAOs reimbursed $16.7M, of which $12.2M was wired overseas. Buzolin was arrested in LA attempting to flee to Moscow. Scheme ran Aug 2025-Jan 2026.

actions.title: CMS Notifies 103,000 Medicare Beneficiaries of Account Fraud Scheme Using Stolen Identities
actions.description: CMS identified suspicious unauthorized creation of Medicare.gov accounts using personal information obtained from unknown external sources. Approximately 103,000 beneficiaries were potentially impacted. Fraudsters used stolen identity data to create beneficiary online accounts, potentially accessing personal health information and Medicare benefits.

actions.title: DOJ: Two Charged in $227M Medicare Fraud Scheme Using Fake COVID-19 Test Kit Labs
actions.description: Syed Murtuza Kablazada, 34, and Syed Mehdi Hussain, 32, of Illinois, charged with operating purported medical laboratories that submitted $227M in fraudulent Medicare claims for over-the-counter COVID-19 test kits. Used foreign nationals as nominee owners. Paid marketers for names of hundreds of thousands of Medicare beneficiaries, including deceased individuals. Medicare paid approximately $136M.

actions.title: President Trump Establishes Task Force to Eliminate Fraud via Executive Order
actions.description: President Trump signed an executive order establishing the Task Force to Eliminate Fraud, chaired by Vice President J.D. Vance, to coordinate federal efforts against fraud, waste, and abuse in federal benefit programs including Medicaid. The task force will develop minimum anti-fraud requirements including identity verification, documentation standards, pre-payment risk controls, and audit procedures. The EO specifically cites Medicaid vulnerabilities in Minnesota, California, Illinois, New York, Maine, and Colorado, and references the Feeding Our Future scheme ($250M stolen) and Minnesota Medicaid fraud (potentially billions). The FTC Chairman serves as Vice Chair, with cabinet secretaries and agency heads as members.

actions.title: Head of the Criminal Division Matthew R. Galeotti Announces Results of Health Care Fraud Takedown
actions.description: Thank you all for joining us today to announce the results of the largest coordinated health care fraud takedown in the history of the Department of Justice.

actions.title: Safe Chain Solutions Owners Sentenced to 38 Combined Years for $92M Black-Market HIV Drug Scheme
actions.description: Patrick Boyd, 47, and Charles Boyd, 43, of Easton, Maryland, were sentenced to 18 and 20 years in prison respectively for distributing more than $92.8 million of black-market HIV drugs through their pharmaceutical wholesale company Safe Chain Solutions. The brothers bought HIV medications from black-market suppliers through unlawful buy-back arrangements, removed or altered original prescription labels, and falsified documentation to make products appear legitimate before reselling them into the legitimate pharmaceutical supply chain. Co-owner Adam Brosius previously pleaded guilty and was sentenced to 97 months. Forfeiture of $21.85 million was ordered. Prosecuted in the Southern District of Florida.

actions.title: San Diego Medical Center Shut Down: Kim Huynh Charged with $11M Medicare Fraud, Practicing Medicine Without a License
actions.description: San Diego County DA, FBI, and HHS-OIG shut down Tubi Connect Inc., a neurofeedback clinic operated by Kim Huynh, 51, who was arrested for billing Medicare and insurers more than $11 million for tests and treatments not covered or not provided. Huynh directed billers to submit claims for EEG scans (used to diagnose epilepsy/seizures) rather than the QEEG scans her clinic actually performed, because QEEGs lack Medicare/insurance coverage. Unlicensed technicians performed scans. Medicare was billed over $10 million ($4.6M paid); private insurers billed $600K+ ($100K+ paid). Huynh also charged with money laundering and practicing medicine without a license. Bail set at $750,000.

actions.title: Anchorage Doctor Sentenced to Prison for Multi-Million-Dollar Health Care Fraud and Tax Evasion
actions.description: An Anchorage doctor was sentenced yesterday to six and a half years in prison and three years of supervised release for executing a $12.5 million health care fraud scheme and evading over $4 million in taxes on the profits of that 15-year scheme. Her husband and co-defendant was sentenced to three years of probation, with two years to be served in home confinement.

actions.title: Owner of Now-Closed Milwaukee Prenatal Care Coordination Company Sentenced to 121 Months’ Imprisonment for Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: The owner of a now-closed Milwaukee prenatal care coordination company was sentenced to 121 months (over 10 years) in federal prison for a health care fraud scheme involving the submission of false claims for prenatal care coordination services to Wisconsin Medicaid.

actions.title: Louisiana Physician Sentenced to 87 Months for Illegally Distributing 1.8 Million Opioid Doses
actions.description: Louisiana physician Dr. Adrian Talbot sentenced to 87 months in prison for illegally distributing over 1.8 million doses of Schedule II controlled substances and defrauding health care programs of $5.4 million. Talbot pre-signed opioid prescriptions for patients he never examined.

actions.title: Four Pharmacists Sentenced for $13M Medicare/Medicaid Prescription Fraud in Michigan and Ohio
actions.description: Four pharmacists sentenced (2 to 10 years) for billing Medicare, Medicaid, and Blue Cross Blue Shield of Michigan over $13 million for prescription medications never dispensed at five pharmacies across Michigan and Ohio.

actions.title: McKinsey & Company Pays $650M to Resolve Criminal and Civil Opioid Fraud Charges
actions.description: McKinsey entered a deferred prosecution agreement for conspiring with Purdue Pharma to aid in misbranding OxyContin. The $650M resolution includes $323M for FCA violations. First time a consulting firm was held criminally accountable for health care fraud.

actions.title: Kimberly-Clark Pays $40.4M for Selling Adulterated MicroCool Surgical Gowns
actions.description: Kimberly-Clark agreed to pay up to $40.4 million via a deferred prosecution agreement for selling millions of adulterated surgical gowns. An employee conducted fraudulent testing to avoid submitting a new 510(k) premarket notification to FDA.

actions.title: DEA Operation Profit Over Patients: 51 Arrests, 15 Million Pills of Diverted Opioids
actions.description: DEA executed Operation Profit Over Patients as part of the National Health Care Fraud Takedown, resulting in 51 arrests and 122 criminal charges against 74 individuals (44 licensed medical professionals) for diverting over 15 million pills of prescription opioids.
