actions.title: Four Pensacola Women Plead Guilty to Unlawful Diversion of Controlled Substances
actions.description: Four Pensacola, Florida women pleaded guilty to unlawful diversion of controlled substances, including conspiracy to distribute oxycodone, hydrocodone, and amphetamine.

actions.title: Florida Research Facility Owners Plead Guilty in Asthma Clinical Trial Fraud
actions.description: Angela Baquero and Ricardo Acuna, owners of A&R Research Group in Pembroke Pines, Florida, pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit wire fraud for submitting fabricated data in asthma drug trials to drug sponsors and ultimately the FDA.

actions.title: Washington Sleep Clinic Owner Pleads Guilty to Selling Adulterated CPAP Devices
actions.description: A physician and owner of a Washington state sleep clinic pleaded guilty to adulterating and misbranding medical devices. He purchased at least 500 used and recalled Philips CPAP/BiPAP machines, directed staff to remove defective foam, and distributed them to patients.

actions.title: Teva Pharmaceuticals Pays $450M to Resolve Copaxone Kickback Allegations
actions.description: Teva Pharmaceuticals agreed to pay $450 million to resolve FCA and Anti-Kickback allegations. For nearly a decade, Teva funneled hundreds of millions to co-pay assistance foundations structured to benefit only Copaxone while steadily raising the drug's price to increase Medicare reimbursements.

actions.title: CVS Omnicare Ordered to Pay $949M for Dispensing Drugs Without Valid Prescriptions
actions.description: A federal judge ordered CVS Omnicare to pay $948.8 million for dispensing drugs to elderly and disabled people in long-term care facilities without valid prescriptions. Omnicare filed over 3.3 million false claims between 2010 and 2018.

actions.title: CVS Caremark Ordered to Pay $290M for Defrauding Medicare Part D Program
actions.description: A federal court ordered CVS Caremark to pay nearly $290 million for deliberately manipulating prescription drug cost data to overcharge Medicare Part D. Originated from whistleblower Sarah Behnke, former Aetna actuary.

actions.title: FDA Issues 200+ Enforcement Letters in DTC Prescription Drug Advertising Crackdown
actions.description: Following a White House memorandum, FDA dramatically increased enforcement of direct-to-consumer prescription drug advertising, issuing over 200 enforcement letters in 2025 including 74 to pharma/biologic manufacturers. Only 5 letters were issued before the September 9 directive.

actions.title: Martinsville Doctor Convicted on 467 Federal Counts of Opioid Distribution
actions.description: Dr. Joel Smithers of Martinsville, VA convicted of 466 counts of illegally prescribing Schedule II controlled substances and maintaining drug-involved premises. Smithers prescribed controlled substances to every patient, distributing over 500,000 Schedule II pills. Sentenced to 40 years.

actions.title: New Haven Psychiatrist Pays $455K, Excluded 20 Years for Unnecessary Opioid Prescriptions
actions.description: Dr. Naimetulla Ahmed Syed of New Haven, CT agreed to pay $455,439 to resolve allegations he issued medically unnecessary controlled substance prescriptions including the 'holy trinity' combination of opioids, benzodiazepines, and muscle relaxants, and billed Medicare and Medicaid for unnecessary visits. Agreed to 20-year exclusion from federal health care programs.

actions.title: North Carolina Doctor Sentenced for $2M Opioid and Unnecessary Drug Testing Fraud
actions.description: Dr. Wendell Lewis Randall, owner of the National Institute of Toxicology in Mt. Airy, NC, sentenced to 30 months for prescribing opioids without medical necessity and requiring all patients to submit to unnecessary urine drug tests, billing Medicare for $753K and Medicaid for $1.3M in fraudulent claims.

actions.title: D.C. Physician Sentenced to 18 Years for Operating Nationwide Opioid Pill Mill
actions.description: Dr. Ndubuski Joseph Okafor sentenced to 18 years for operating a pill mill from his D.C. clinic, prescribing opioids to individuals using false identities across at least 45 states, resulting in hundreds of thousands of opioid units prescribed.

actions.title: Houston Pill Mill Pharmacy Owner Sentenced to 12 Years; 500,000+ Opioid Pills Dispensed
actions.description: Arthur Billings, owner of Health Fit Pharmacy (a cash-only pill mill in Houston), sentenced to 12 years and ordered to forfeit $2.6 million. Three pharmacists also sentenced. The pharmacy dispensed over 500,000 opioid pills to individuals sent by drug traffickers, using prescriptions written in names of physicians whose identities were stolen.

actions.title: Miami Pharmaceutical Wholesaler Admits Selling 14 Million Opioid Doses to Pill Mill Pharmacies
actions.description: Atlantic Biologicals Corporation entered a deferred prosecution agreement admitting its unit National Apothecary Solutions sold over 14 million opioid doses to Houston-area pill mill pharmacies from 2017 to 2023, knowing they would be dispensed outside legitimate medical practice. Paid $450,000 criminal penalty.

actions.title: DOJ Sues Stockton Nurse Practitioner for Selling Opioid Prescriptions via Telegram
actions.description: DOJ filed a civil complaint against nurse practitioner Joan Rubinger of Stockton, CA for a nationwide scheme to sell illegal opioid prescriptions for cash via Telegram. Rubinger issued over 900 illegitimate prescriptions without examinations, providing customers price lists to select their own drugs.

actions.title: Great Neck, NY Doctor Sentenced to 7 Years for Operating Oxycodone Pill Mill
actions.description: Dr. Roya Jafari-Hassad of Great Neck, NY sentenced to 7 years for operating an oxycodone pill mill from her family practice, making hundreds of thousands of dollars selling prescriptions under the table from 2019 to 2022. Ordered to pay $150,000 fine and $152,765 in restitution.

actions.title: House E&C Hearing: Protecting Patients and Safeguarding Taxpayer Dollars — The Role of CMS in Combatting Medicare and Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: House Energy & Commerce Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations held a hearing on the Trump Administration's efforts to proactively tackle Medicare and Medicaid fraud, with testimony on CMS enforcement actions, the CRUSH initiative, and vulnerable programs targeted by fraud schemes.

actions.title: House E&C Launches Investigation into Ongoing Medicaid Fraud in Minnesota
actions.description: House Energy & Commerce Committee leaders launched a formal investigation into ongoing Medicaid fraud in Minnesota, requesting documents from Governor Walz and the Department of Human Services. The fraud scheme, potentially operating since 2013, involves overbilling, false records, identity theft, and phantom claims.

actions.title: House Oversight Launches Investigation of Fraud in New York's Medicaid Programs
actions.description: Chairman Comer launched an investigation into allegations that New York State misrepresented federal Medicaid fund sources involving Nassau University Medical Center. The alleged scheme has reportedly cost taxpayers over $1 billion over 20+ years. Comer requested a staff briefing from CMS Administrator Dr. Oz.

actions.title: House Oversight Widens Investigation into Minnesota Social Services and Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: Chairman Comer widened the investigation into extensive money laundering and fraud in Minnesota's social services programs, following findings by the U.S. Attorney for the District of Minnesota. A legislative audit revealed a grantee receiving nearly $680,000 for a single month without documentation.

actions.title: H.R. 1784: Medicare Fraud Detection and Deterrence Act of 2025 Introduced
actions.description: Bill introduced requiring CMS to deactivate standard unique health identifiers of health care providers excluded from federal programs due to fraud, preventing excluded providers from continuing to bill Medicare.

actions.title: S. 3593: Bill to Increase Health Care Fraud Penalties Introduced in Senate
actions.description: Senate bill introduced to increase criminal and civil penalties for health care fraud convictions and to strengthen enforcement tools available to federal prosecutors.

actions.title: CMS Defers $259M in Federal Medicaid Payments to Minnesota, Freezes Provider Enrollment
actions.description: CMS temporarily deferred $259 million in federal Medicaid payments to Minnesota for FY2025 claims and froze provider enrollment. Payments deferred for 14 high-risk programs worth $3.75 billion annually, marking an aggressive new federal approach to state Medicaid fraud.

actions.title: Senate Finance Hearing: The President's 2026 Health Care Agenda — HHS Secretary Kennedy Testifies on Fraud Prevention
actions.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. testified before Senate Finance. Chairman Crapo questioned Kennedy on fraud prevention. CMS discovered 2.8 million Americans enrolled in duplicate Medicaid or ACA Exchange plans, with projected savings of $14 billion annually from removing them.

actions.title: Senate HELP Hearing: Restoring Integrity — Preventing Fraud in Child Care Assistance Programs
actions.description: Chairman Cassidy convened a hearing examining rampant fraud in federal child care funding, triggered by Minnesota's scandal where 1 in 10 child care dollars was stolen or misused. Followed Cassidy's demand for documents from Governor Walz.

actions.title: Six Committee Chairmen Request HHS-OIG Briefing on LA County Hospice and Home Health Fraud
actions.description: Six chairmen from House E&C and Ways & Means sent a letter to HHS-OIG requesting a briefing on hospice fraud in LA County. Auditors found 112 hospices at the same address, $1.2 billion in improper home health payments, and $198 million in suspected hospice fraud. Armenian organized crime networks identified as key players.

actions.title: House Judiciary Subpoenas 8 Major Health Insurers Over ACA Enrollment Fraud
actions.description: Subpoenas issued to Blue Shield of California, Centene, CVS Health, Elevance Health, GuideWell, HCSC, Kaiser Permanente, and Oscar Health demanding information on fraud-protection measures, enrollees with unused subsidies, broker payments, and internal fraud audits.

actions.title: DOGE Releases Largest Medicaid Dataset in History to Crowdsource Fraud Detection
actions.description: DOGE released aggregated provider-level Medicaid claims data covering fee-for-service, managed care, and CHIP claims from 2018-2024 to crowdsource fraud detection. DOGE gained access to CMS systems in February 2025.

actions.title: GAO 2025 High-Risk List: Medicare and Medicaid Maintain High-Risk Status; 129 Open Recommendations
actions.description: GAO biennial report identified 38 high-risk areas. Medicare and Medicaid maintained high-risk status with 64 and 65 open recommendations respectively, including improving hospice oversight, addressing Medicare Advantage risks, and strengthening Medicaid provider screening. Programs on the list represent 80% of government-wide improper payments.

actions.title: GAO Report: Medicaid Managed Care Improper Payment Estimate Near 0% Despite Known Fraud Risks
actions.description: GAO raised concerns that Medicaid managed care's near-0% improper payment estimate masks real fraud risks. Between October 2021 and February 2025, CMS audits identified $33 million in overpayments. About 75% of Medicaid beneficiaries (74 million people) receive coverage through managed care.

actions.title: Senate Finance Chair Crapo Applauds CMS Finding 2.8M Duplicate Medicaid/ACA Enrollments
actions.description: Chairman Crapo praised CMS after the agency found 2.8 million Americans enrolled in both Medicaid and ACA Exchange plans simultaneously. Eliminating duplicate enrollment projected to save $14 billion annually.

actions.title: MedPAC/CRFB Analysis: Medicare Advantage Upcoding to Cost $40B in 2025; $1.3 Trillion Over Decade
actions.description: MedPAC found upcoding will increase Medicare payments to private insurers by an estimated 10% ($40 billion) in 2025. CRFB estimated MA overpayments of $1.3 trillion over the next decade. CMS Administrator Oz pledged to go after upcoding during his Senate confirmation.

actions.title: S. 2066: Medicare Transaction Fraud Prevention Act — Pilot Program for Predictive Risk Scoring
actions.description: Bill establishing a 2-year pilot program to test predictive risk-scoring algorithms for oversight of Medicare payments for durable medical equipment and clinical lab tests. Sponsored by Senator Tim Sheehy.

actions.title: H.R. 7155: Stop Fraud in Federal Programs Act of 2026
actions.description: Increases penalties for theft or bribery concerning programs receiving federal funds. Also requires audits under the summer food service program. Referred to Committees on Judiciary and Education and Workforce.

actions.title: H.R. 7677: Closing the Provider Fraud Gap Act — Passed Committee 35-0
actions.description: Requires the Comptroller General to study fraud prevention in federal early childhood education, child care, and child nutrition programs. Passed House Education and Workforce Committee 35-0 on March 5, 2026.

actions.title: Ending Improper Payments to Deceased People Act Passes Senate HSGAC Unanimously
actions.description: Permanently amends the Social Security Act to allow SSA to share the Death Master File with Treasury's Do Not Pay system, preventing improper payments to deceased individuals. The Treasury recovered $31 million in fraud during the prior version's implementation.

actions.title: HHS-OIG Audit: Mississippi Failed to Report $4.5M in MFCU Medicaid Overpayments
actions.description: Federal audit found Mississippi failed to report and return MFCU-determined Medicaid overpayments totaling $4.5 million ($3.7M federal share) across 20 cases in FY 2021-2023. OIG recommended Mississippi return $3.5M in federal share.

actions.title: Mississippi Orthopedic Clinic Pays $1.87M for Billing Compound Drugs as Brand-Name Products
actions.description: Mitias Orthopaedics and its owner agreed to pay $1.87 million to settle allegations of injecting Medicare/Medicaid beneficiaries with drugs purchased from compound pharmacies while billing as FDA-approved, brand-name products.

actions.title: Idaho AmeriHealth Clinics Consents to $2M Judgment for Using Vulnerable Staff to Submit False Claims
actions.description: AmeriHealth Clinics consented to a $2 million judgment for False Claims Act violations involving a scheme using vulnerable and inexperienced medical staff to submit false claims to Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE.

actions.title: Bismarck Man Charged with $1.8M Government Fraud Including False Medicaid Claims
actions.description: A Bismarck, North Dakota man was charged with taking $1.8 million through government fraud, including fraudulent Medicaid claims submitted between January 2022 and September 2024. Case brought as part of the 2025 National Health Care Fraud Takedown.

actions.title: Home Health Care Operator Admits Defrauding Missouri Medicaid
actions.description: Camille S. Childress, 41, of St. Louis County, pleaded guilty to one count of health care fraud for defrauding Missouri Medicaid through her home health care company, Inspiring Angels LLC. Childress concealed her ownership of the company due to a 2012 criminal conviction and submitted fraudulent claims for home health care services that were never provided. Missouri Medicaid paid at least $174,496 on false claims submitted in 2021 and 2022, though Childress was originally accused of defrauding the program out of more than $3 million over more than a decade. She faces up to 10 years in prison and fines up to $250,000, with sentencing scheduled for June 16, 2026.

actions.title: Center for Vein Restoration Agrees to Pay $4 Million to Resolve False Claims Act Allegations
actions.description: CVR Management, LLC, doing business as Center for Vein Restoration, Center for Vascular Medicine, and Dr. Sanjiv Lakhanpal agreed to pay $4 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that they billed Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE for medically unnecessary vein treatment procedures.

actions.title: Azerbaijani National Charged in $90 Million Medicare Advantage Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Anar Rustamov, a national of Azerbaijan who appears to have entered the U.S. illegally, was indicted and charged with 14 counts of health care fraud and money laundering for orchestrating a scheme involving thousands of false claims for medical equipment (blood glucose monitors, orthotic braces) submitted to Medicare Advantage programs, totaling over $90 million. Rustamov operated through an entity called Dublin Helping Hand. He remains at large and faces up to 20 years in prison and $250,000 fine per count.

actions.title: Dominican National Pleads Guilty to Health Care Fraud, Wire Fraud, and False SSN Charges
actions.description: William Jose Alejandro Arias Amador, a Dominican national, pleaded guilty to false representation of a Social Security number, health care fraud, and wire fraud. Sentencing is scheduled for July 8, 2026.

actions.title: House Oversight Committee Launches Investigation into California Hospice Fraud, Sends Letter to Gov. Newsom
actions.description: House Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer launched an investigation into rampant hospice fraud in California, sending a letter to Gov. Newsom requesting documents on the state's oversight and internal controls. Key findings: LA County saw a 1,500% increase in hospice providers since 2010, with at least $105 million in annual Medicare overbilling. CMS estimates LA County hospice fraud totals $3.5 billion, with 18% of all U.S. hospice billing originating from LA County alone.

actions.title: Pakistani National Residing in Southern California Charged with Fraudulently Billing Medicare Plans
actions.description: The owner of Oregon Clinical Laboratory in Klamath Falls, Oregon, has been charged by criminal complaint with health care fraud after allegedly participating in a scheme to fraudulently bill Medicare Advantage insurance plans for laboratory testing services never performed.

actions.title: Alabama Doctor Sentenced to Over One Year in Prison for $2.7 Million Telemedicine Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Tommie Robinson, a 44-year-old Alabama doctor, was sentenced to 16 months in prison for operating a telemedicine fraud scheme that defrauded health care programs of $2.7 million. Robinson submitted claims for medically unnecessary durable medical equipment and genetic testing through telemedicine services. He pleaded guilty to one count of health care fraud and was ordered to pay $2,784,733.49 in restitution.

actions.title: Florida Doctor Pleads Guilty to Making False Statements in Connection with Multi-Million-Dollar Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Simon Grinshteyn, 52, of Florida, pleaded guilty to one count of making false statements relating to health care matters. Grinshteyn signed orders for medically unnecessary genetic testing and durable medical equipment without having any direct provider-patient relationship, causing Medicare to pay over $3.1 million in fraudulent claims. Sentencing is scheduled for June 24, 2026.

actions.title: Mississippi Man Ordered to Pay $31 Million for Role in Health Care Kickback Scheme
actions.description: Robert L. Crites, 67, of Batesville, Mississippi, was ordered to pay $31,039,134.82 in a federal judgment for orchestrating an illegal kickback scheme. Crites, through his companies Health Services Plus and TriCom LLC, identified and referred patients to Cloverland Pharmacy in Alabama, primarily targeting TRICARE beneficiaries. The pharmacy compensated Crites for each referral in violation of the Anti-Kickback Statute and False Claims Act.

actions.title: Consent Judgment Entered Against Bucks County Company Resolving Allegations of False Claims for Billing Group Art Classes in Assisted Living and Adult Day Facilities as Occupational Therapy
actions.description: A consent judgment was entered against Segal Arts LLC and owner Irina Segal of Bucks County, Pennsylvania, for submitting false Medicare claims for one-on-one occupational therapy services that were actually group art-and-crafts sessions at assisted living and adult day facilities in Pennsylvania and New Jersey. The company billed Medicare for medically necessary therapeutic exercises that were never delivered.

actions.title: Owner of Now-Closed Milwaukee Prenatal Care Coordination Company Sentenced to 60 Months’ Imprisonment for Health Care Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Lakia Jackson, 36, was sentenced to 60 months in federal prison for a health care fraud scheme that stole over $2.5 million from Wisconsin Medicaid's prenatal care coordination program. Jackson offered women kickbacks in exchange for their Medicaid identification numbers and then falsely billed the state for prenatal care services that were minimally provided or never rendered. She was ordered to forfeit and pay restitution of $2,361,799.17.

actions.title: Congress to Investigate Hospice Fraud
actions.description: The U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Oversight and Government Reform has launched an investigation into “rampant” hospice fraud in California. Committee members wrote to California Governor Gavin Newsom (D) requesting documents and communications regarding the state’s oversight and internal controls for detecting or preventing fraud. “In California, your administration’s Departments of Public Health, Social […] The post Congress to Investigate Hospice Fraud appeared first on Hospice News .

actions.title: HHS-OIG: Medicaid Fraud Control Units Annual Report FY2025 — $2 Billion in Combined Recoveries
actions.description: HHS-OIG released its annual report on Medicaid Fraud Control Units (MFCUs), reporting combined criminal and civil recoveries of nearly $2 billion for fiscal year 2025. Criminal recoveries from convictions totaled $1.3 billion and civil recoveries totaled $706 million across all state MFCUs.

actions.title: Telemedicine Company Owner Pleads Guilty to $46M Medicare Fraud Scheme
actions.description: The owner of a telemedicine company pleaded guilty today to organizing and leading a $46.2 million Medicare fraud conspiracy that spanned more than six years. According to court documents, Christopher Harwood, 43, of Fort Lauderdale, Florida, admitted that he owned and operated a telemedicine company called TelevisitMD. Harwood and his co-conspirators targeted Medicare patients through aggressive telemarketing campaigns, inducing them to accept orthotic braces and genetic tests that they did not need. Harwood paid doctors to approve orders for these braces and genetic tests.

actions.title: OB/GYN Physician Agrees to Pay $507,500 to Resolve False Claims Act Allegations Connected to Fraudulent Prescription Scheme
actions.description: Baltimore, Maryland – A Maryland gynecologist agreed to a settlement in connection with federal False Claims Act violation allegations. Valinda R. Nwadike, MD, agreed to pay the United States $507,500 to resolve allegations for her role in a telemarketing scheme designed to defraud Medicare and TRICARE. U.S. Attorney Kelly O.

actions.title: Federal Jury Finds Mother and Daughter Guilty of Medicare Fraud Scheme and Payment of Illegal Kickbacks
actions.description: SAN DIEGO – A federal jury today convicted a mother and daughter from Melbourne, Florida, of multiple counts of health care fraud, payment of illegal kickbacks, and conspiracy to commit those offenses. The mother, Cindy Justice, was the owner and president of PureScience Rx, a pharmacy located in Poway. The daughter, Ashleigh Davis, was the operations manager at PureScience Rx and was a licensed pharmacy technician.

actions.title: Team Rehab Physical Therapy Agrees to Pay Nearly $5 Million To Resolve False Claims Act Allegations Related to Fraudulent Billing Scheme
actions.description: DETROIT - United States Attorney Jerome F. Gorgon Jr. announced today that Team Rehabilitation Services, LLC (“Team Rehab”), which operates approximately 140 physical therapy clinics in Michigan, Illinois, Indiana, Wisconsin, and Georgia, has agreed to pay $4,969,494 to settle allegations that it violated the False Claims Act by submitting false claims for payment for physical therapy services to several federal health care programs. Read more on www.justice.gov Action Details Date: March 25, 2026 Agency: U.S.

actions.title: Texas Fugitive Apprehended and Sentenced to Over 12 Years in Prison for $61M Telemarketing Fraud Scheme Targeting Medicare Beneficiaries
actions.description: A Texas man was sentenced Wednesday to 150 months in prison and two years of supervised release for organizing and leading a $61.5 million health care fraud and wire fraud conspiracy in which thousands of Medicare beneficiaries who were the victims of deceptive telemarketing were sent thousands of orthotic braces, foot baths, and genetic tests they did not need.

actions.title: Larchmont Woman Sentenced to Nearly 3 Years in Federal Prison for Her Role in Hospice and Diagnostic Testing Fraud that Conned Medicare
actions.description: LOS ANGELES – A woman from the Larchmont area of Los Angeles was sentenced today to 35 months in federal prison for defrauding Medicare out of more than $14 million by submitting fraudulent claims for hospice care and diagnostic testing services that were either unnecessary or not provided at all. Sophia Shaklian, 38, was sentenced by United States District Judge Stanley Blumenfeld Jr., who also ordered her to pay $14,103,043 in restitution. Shaklian pleaded guilty in November 2025 to one count of health care fraud.
